p: An educational web project designed for people who want to learn or refresh their knowledge of first aid and emergency care, particularly when preparing for a driver's license.
A web project designed to help people with disabilities find accessible places and feel more confident when planning their journeys.
A mobile-first web application designed for cleaners and cleaning companies to keep track of restroom cleaning schedules.
An interactive web project created as a personalized gift in the form of a clicker game.
p2: The project combines short learning materials with interactive quizzes to make information easy to understand and remember. A points system encourages users to return regularly, making the learning experience more engaging and motivating.

The project is built around an interactive map that provides detailed accessibility information about locations, including ramps, stairs, parking, step-free access, and other important features. It focuses on accessibility, inclusive design, and improving the overall user experience.

Users can quickly record the latest cleaning session and view the three previous entries, making it easy to monitor cleaning history. The project was developed as part of a university assignment to create a simple digital solution for a common everyday problem, with a strong focus on usability and mobile design.

The project combines game mechanics with web development, allowing users to compete for the highest score on a leaderboard. It showcases my ability to create interactive web applications that use gamification to engage users.
